coverage_note: The bundled SYCE3 population database is literature oriented and does not include a complete allele-frequency panel. Because confirmed human SYCE3 pathogenic mutations have not yet been established in the cited reviews, interpretation should prioritize current ClinVar/gnomAD status, predicted consequence, reproductive phenotype, and independent confirmation.
gene_population_patterns_intro: Broader population patterns curated from SYCE3 synaptonemal-complex, testis-expression, and meiotic-infertility model literature.

sources.label: NCBI Gene 644186: SYCE3 expression and meiotic GO context
Human Protein Atlas: SYCE3 testis and spermatocyte expression context
PMCID PMC3102746: Syce3 knockout mouse fertility model
PMCID PMC6556580: SYCE3 structural self-assembly model
PMCID PMC12676892: NOA review covering SYCE2/SYCE3 model phenotypes

gene_population_patterns.summary: NCBI and Human Protein Atlas resources frame SYCE3 as testis-enriched, especially in spermatocyte and spermatid meiotic cell states.
Mouse Syce3 deficiency supports a meiotic-arrest and fertility research thesis, but it should not be treated as a validated human diagnostic gene-disease rule.
Structural work supports SYCE3 coiled-coil self-assembly as a mechanistic layer for synaptonemal-complex central-element lattice formation.
Bundled EPIC probes provide local promoter and gene-body methylation context, but no population methylation threshold is bundled for meiotic arrest, NOA, POI, or recurrent pregnancy loss.
